title: campylospermum laevigatum
intro: Campylospermum laevigatum is a plant species that belongs to the family Ochnaceae, commonly known as the chaff flower. It is a woody shrub or small tree that is native to the tropical regions of Africa. The medicinal properties of Campylospermum laevigatum include its ability to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant can be prepared as an herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and has been reported to provide health benefits such as relieving pain and reducing inflammation.
